title: Bonjour ! — Greetings & introducing yourself
objective: Greet people, say your name, and use basic courtesy words (A1: can use familiar everyday expressions and introduce oneself).

title: Ma famille — Family & possessives
objective: Name family members and say what belongs to you using possessives (A1: can describe family and immediate surroundings).

title: Les nombres et l'heure — Numbers, days & time
objective: Count, ask and tell the time, and name the days of the week (A1: can handle numbers, quantities and time).

title: Au restaurant — Food & drink
objective: Order food and drink and say what you like (A1: can order a meal and express simple preferences).

title: Les verbes en -er — Regular present tense
objective: Describe everyday actions using regular -er verbs (A1: can describe habits and routines in simple terms).

title: Être, avoir, aller, faire — Key irregular verbs
objective: Use the four essential irregular verbs in the present (A1: can use high-frequency verbs to handle basic needs).

title: Questions et négation — Asking & saying no
objective: Ask simple questions and make negative statements (A1: can ask and answer simple questions on familiar topics).

title: En ville — Places & directions
objective: Locate places and give simple directions (A1: can ask for and give directions using a map or in the street).

title: Le futur proche — Near future & plans
objective: Say what you are going to do in the near future (A1: can make and describe simple plans).

title: Ma journée — Daily routine
objective: Describe a typical day and how often you do things (A1: can describe daily routines and habits simply).

title: Quel temps fait-il ? — Weather & small talk
objective: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).

title: Les vêtements et les couleurs — Clothes & colours
objective: Describe clothes and colours with correct adjective agreement (A1: can describe everyday objects and clothing with simple adjectives).

title: Le passé composé (avoir) — Talking about the past
objective: Recount recent past events using the passé composé with avoir (A2: can describe past activities and personal experiences).

title: Le passé composé (être) — Movement verbs
objective: Narrate past events involving movement, with correct agreement (A2: can recount a journey or trip).

title: L'imparfait — Describing the past
objective: Describe past situations, habits and how things used to be (A2: can describe past routines and backgrounds).

title: Les verbes pronominaux — Reflexive verbs
objective: Describe your daily routine in detail using reflexive verbs (A2: can describe routines and habits).

title: Faire les courses — Shopping & comparatives
objective: Shop, ask quantities, and compare items (A2: can handle shopping and make simple comparisons).

title: Les pronoms COD/COI — Object pronouns
objective: Replace nouns with object pronouns to avoid repetition (A2: can use pronouns in connected speech).

title: Les projets — Plans & the future
objective: Talk about future plans and intentions (A2: can describe plans, hopes and intentions).

title: Donner son opinion — Giving opinions
objective: Express and justify simple opinions (A2: can give and explain opinions on familiar topics).

title: Chez le médecin — Health & the body
objective: Describe symptoms, name body parts and give simple advice. (A2: can describe symptoms and follow or give simple health advice.)

title: Au travail — Jobs & the workplace
objective: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)

title: Se loger — Housing & furniture
objective: Describe your home, its rooms and furniture. (A2: can describe in simple terms one's living conditions.)

title: Raconter un voyage — Telling a travel story
objective: Narrate a past trip, combining events with descriptions (A2: can tell a simple connected story about a past experience).

title: Object pronouns
objective: Using le, la, les, lui, leur
